payload.title: TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
payload.detailsBlock: During routine monitoring, the freezer was found not functioning properly and was unable to maintain the required storage temperature. The temperature exceeded the specified limit, resulting in a deviation from food safety and storage requirements.
payload.correctiveAction: Informed Maintenance team immediate repair for the freezer.

payload.title: TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
payload.detailsBlock:
During inspection, the floor in the chiller and production area was found damaged and cracked in several locations. The damaged surface may cause accumulation of water, dirt, and food residues, creating a potential contamination and hygiene risk.
This condition does not comply with GMP and HACCP requirements for maintaining food processing areas in good hygienic condition.
Evidence Observed
Floor surface cracked and damaged
Uneven floor condition observed in chiller area
Difficulty in proper cleaning and sanitation
Risk of water stagnation and contamination

payload.title: TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
payload.rootCause: the food was kept inside the chiller for returning to QCS branch, it may chance to cross contamination
payload.detailsBlock: Food items were found contaminated inside chiller .Raw and contaminated food found in same chiller ,it may chance to cross contamination
payload.correctiveAction: informed executive chef for remove or transfer immediately from the chiller

payload.title: TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
payload.rootCause: I allowed the customer to collect and transport the chilled meat using his private non-refrigerated car, upon his request and under the customer’s own responsibility, instead of strictly enforcing the company requirement to use only approved refrigerated vehicles.
payload.detailsBlock: Meat consignment was transported in a private passenger car (saloon) without any temperature control or basic hygiene conditions, which is not in line with company procedures and halal / food safety transport requirements (cold chain and proper meat transportation conditions).
payload.correctiveAction: Immediately stopped allowing loading of meat consignments into private non-refrigerated cars, and instructed staff that all chilled/frozen meat must only be dispatched through approved clean refrigerated vehicles with temperature control. Staff will be briefed and re-trained on meat transportation, cold-chain, and halal requirements, and the procedure will be updated to clearly prohibit such practice and to require vehicle verification before loading.

payload.title: TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
payload.rootCause:
Poor cooling performance due to low refrigerant gas, compressor inefficiency, or blocked condenser/evaporator coils.

Frequent door opening or improper door sealing causing cold air loss.

Overloading the chiller beyond its capacity, restricting air circulation.

Inaccurate thermostat setting or temperature probe malfunction.

Delayed preventive maintenance or cleaning of coils/filters.
payload.detailsBlock: CHILLER AND DISPLAY CHILLER TEMPERATURE FOUND ABOVE 5C
payload.correctiveAction:
Immediate Action:

Transfer temperature-sensitive products to another functional chiller.

Reduce load and ensure proper air circulation inside the unit.

Close doors properly and limit opening frequency.

Technical Action:

Call the maintenance team to check for refrigerant leaks, compressor performance, and electrical faults.

Clean condenser and evaporator coils.

Verify thermostat calibration and replace faulty sensors if needed.

Repair or replace damaged door gaskets/seals.

Preventive Action:

Implement a daily temperature monitoring and logging system.

Schedule monthly preventive maintenance for all chillers.

Train staff on proper loading practices and door-usage discipline.

payload.title: TRANS EMIRATES LIVESTOCK LLC • NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
payload.rootCause:
Continuous heavy trolley/pallet movement without proper floor protection.

Lack of periodic maintenance.

Moisture seepage causing surface weakening
Impact from heavy materials or equipment during loading.

Use of metal pallets/trolleys that cause abrasion.

Poor bonding of the top floor layer.
Frequent collision with trolleys or product crates entering/exiting the door.

Wear and tear due to age and continuous usage.

Poor alignment of strips, causing stress and breakage.
payload.detailsBlock:
FOUND FLOOR DAMAGED
LOADING FLOOR CHIPOFF
PLASTIC AIR CURTAIN DAMAGED
payload.correctiveAction:
Repair the damaged floor section with appropriate industrial-grade material.

Install protective floor coverings or rubber bumpers in high-traffic areas.

Implement a preventive maintenance schedule (monthly inspection).
Re-screed and resurface the chipped-off areas.

Train staff on safe loading/unloading practices to avoid impact damage.

Use non-metallic or rubber-wheeled trolleys to minimize wear.
Replace the damaged air curtain plastic strips.

Install protective side guards or guides to prevent collision during movement.

Conduct regular inspection and replacement every 6–12 months
